description_long: This object is a delicate, slender-stemmed flower with a single prominent bloom at the top, displaying soft lavender-blue petals radiating outward from a dark central core. The flower is accompanied by two smaller buds on curved stems, suggesting different stages of blooming. The petals are slightly wavy and overlap at their bases, creating a gentle, natural symmetry. The stem is green and slightly curved, with a smooth surface and a few elongated, lanceolate leaves attached near the base and along the stem. The overall appearance is lightweight and fragile, typical of wildflowers found in urban parks or roadside verges. The coloration transitions from deep purple at the flower's center to lighter blue at the petal tips, with green hues dominating the stem and buds.
description: A slender-stemmed flower with lavender-blue petals, a dark center, and two smaller buds on curved stems. The stem is green and smooth, with a few lanceolate leaves.
description_view_0: The main bloom faces forward, showing the full spread of five lavender-blue petals and two buds on curved stems.
description_view_1: The flower is seen from the side, highlighting the curvature of the stem and the overlapping petals, with one bud more prominent.
description_view_2: A frontal view of the flower, displaying the symmetrical arrangement of petals and the dark central core, with both buds visible.
description_view_3: The flower is viewed from the opposite side, emphasizing the back of the petals and the elongated stem, with the leaves more visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with five petals radiating symmetrically from the center and slightly overlapping at their bases, forming a star-like pattern.
